40 years after Chernobyl: In Kazakhstan, the forgotten memory of the 'liquidators'
subhead: In the days following the explosion at the Chernobyl nuclear power plant on April 26, 1986, more than 30,000 Kazakhs were sent to the Ukrainian site to help with the cleanup. Those who survived the radiation are finding it increasingly difficult to be acknowledged as victims, even as their country has embarked on a civilian nuclear program.

title: Syrian court conducts first hearing in trial of Bashar al-Assad
subhead: The ousted ruler and his brother have fled Syria and will be tried in absentia. 'Today we begin the first trials of transitional justice in Syria,' announced the judge as he opened the session.

title: Bruce, the disabled parrot who became king
subhead: Deprived of the upper half of his beak, this New Zealand kea, rescued by the Willowbank Wildlife Reserve in 2013, has developed a unique and unstoppable fighting technique.

title: Should fiction be faithful to history? Film director Xavier Giannoli and historian Tal Bruttmann discuss
subhead: Xavier Giannoli's latest film, 'Les Rayons et les Ombres,' is a critical and box office success. It has also sparked questions about its approach to the character of Jean Luchaire, a collaborator during World War II. Can a work of art take liberties with the facts, especially when dealing with such a sensitive period?

subhead: Over a series of conversations held between 2024 and 2026 with Le Monde, the philosopher and WWII resistant analyzed the contemporary world's upheaval, from the rise of authoritarianism in the West to conflicts in the Middle East, as well as the impact of new technologies and environmental issues.

title: 'Many see Trump's plans to reshape Washington as a metaphor for his governing style'
subhead: In an interview, Lawrence Vale, professor of urban planning at MIT, explains how the American president is striving to leave his mark on the federal capital through initiatives such as the Triumphal Arch, White House ballroom and statues.

title: Weather sensors at Charles de Gaulle Airport at center of suspected manipulation on Polymarket
subhead: On the evenings of April 6 and April 15, temperatures recorded by a sensor at Paris Charles de Gaulle Airport spiked abnormally. Was it a weather phenomenon or an anomaly? There are strong suspicions of manipulation connected to online betting.
